danger_signals.visual_form: Detailed, oversized enemy ships filling screen space
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring focused attention; screen-dominating scale communicates danger level

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible bullet patterns emanating from enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate hazard; density and trajectory communicate incoming damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, angular mechanical enemies with aggressive silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Faction identity for hostile forces; mechanical/industrial aesthetic signals technological enemy

danger_signals.visual_form: Deteriorating planetary terrain and structures as progression advances
danger_signals.meaning: Increasing difficulty and threat level within stage

danger_signals.visual_form: Large stationary structures visible on planets Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis
danger_signals.meaning: Strategic targets; their presence indicates ORN military infrastructure and danger zones

safety_signals.visual_form: Item sprites dropped by defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Power increase; collecting upgrades enhances survivability

safety_signals.visual_form: Wingmate acquisition items that auto-equip to maximum of two
safety_signals.meaning: Protective companion formation; expanded firepower and defensive coverage

safety_signals.visual_form: Ship maneuvering responsiveness controlled by player across four levels
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency in navigation; ability to adapt speed mitigates hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: FIRE LEO-03 Styx ship design and aesthetic
safety_signals.meaning: Allied presence; player ship represents friendly faction and legitimacy

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-controlled ship; small, sleek design that avoids Cerberus detection

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varied enemy ship designs across five planets; planet-specific enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cloaking device installations on five planets; Cerberus defense system; ORN headquarters final stage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unique visual presentation as ultimate threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: ORN headquarters interior; final three stages after planet assault

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Initial mission briefing—five cloaking devices intact
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Five selectable planets displayed; each contains visible cloaking device infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player chooses approach order; enemies defend installations aggressively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unique planetary environments (Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis); distinct visual themes and color palettes per stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy difficulty and composition varies by planet; stage-specific patterns reflect ORN military deployment strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from planetary exteriors to ORN interior headquarters aesthetic; visual shift from organic/terraformed to mechanical/technological
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Three-stage infiltration sequence; enemy patterns intensify; Cerberus presence indicated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bio-computer entity as final boss; fusion of organic and machine aesthetics reflecting hybrid nature
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Attack patterns escalate; screen-filling threat; ultimate danger signal through visual dominance and complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual swap of player ship's firing effect based on equipped weapon (Twin shot, Back shot, or new exclusive weapons)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CLAW wingmates mirror chosen weapon; tactical visual feedback for player loadout decisions

environmental_storytelling.example: Multiple background layers at different scroll speeds create depth perception; detailed intricate backgrounds communicate technological sophistication of ORN territory
Each of five planets (Hydra, Gorgon, Seiren, Haides, Ellis) has distinct visual identity; terrain, color scheme, and architecture tell story of ORN occupation and fortification
Large installed structures visible within each planet's stage; their physical presence on skyline communicates military infrastructure and strategic objective
Different enemy types across stages reflect planetary defenses; visual evolution from outer perimeter to inner headquarters shows escalating threat tier
Final three stages transition to interior mechanical environment; visual departure from planetary exteriors signals narrative progression toward core threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to player input; speed adjustable across four levels; fires selected weapon with CLAW support
Scripted movement patterns; projectile spam in varying densities; large sprites prioritize screen presence to communicate threat
Stationary targets; serve as stage focal points; destruction progression communicates mission advancement
Referenced narratively; presence felt through stage design and enemy intensity during final three stages
Complex attack patterns; screen-filling visual dominance; escalating difficulty phases communicate ultimate threat status

notes: Thunder Force III communicates world-state primarily through visual environment design, enemy sprite scale/variation, and stage progression structure. The Galaxy Federation vs. ORN Empire conflict is conveyed through aesthetic contrast: player ship small and efficient (stealth doctrine), ORN forces large and aggressive (military doctrine). Cloaking devices serve dual purpose: narrative mission objectives and visual danger markers. Parallax scrolling and detailed backgrounds reinforce technological sophistication of encountered territory. Weapon system visual feedback (firing patterns, CLAW mimicry) provides tactical communication layer separate from danger signaling.
